reflection.quote: We are what we repeatedly do. Excellence, then, is not an act, but a habit.
reflection.reading:
It's a Tuesday in July. Nothing special about it. No holiday, no anniversary, no crisis to manage. Just a regular weekday in the middle of the summer, the kind of day that used to disappear into the blur of whatever I was drinking to get through.

These are the days that used to feel wasted on me. I'd look at a random Tuesday and see nothing worth being present for — no reason to feel my feet on the floor, no story I wanted to be inside of. So I'd check out. And then I'd wake up on Wednesday having lost another one. Multiply that by years and you start to understand what I actually gave up: not the parties, not the big nights, but the Tuesdays. Thousands of them. The unremarkable middle of my own life.

What sobriety gave back wasn't the highlight reel — the ski trips, the summits, the flights. Those are real, and they matter. But the deeper gift is this: a Tuesday in July where I make coffee, answer emails, go for a walk, eat dinner with someone I love, and go to bed clear-headed. A day where nothing happened, and I was there for all of it. That's the life. Durant was right — you don't become someone in the big moments. You become them in the repetitions no one is watching.
reflection.meditation: What would it mean to actually show up for today, as ordinary as it is?

affirmations: I am building a life one unremarkable day at a time, and that is the work.
Today I have the clarity to notice what's actually in front of me.
I am someone who can be trusted with a quiet Tuesday.

gratitude.prayer: Grateful for a clear head on a regular day. Grateful for the coffee, the summer light, the fact that I remember yesterday and get to be awake for today. There was a version of me who couldn't have imagined wanting a Tuesday like this — and here I am, wanting it.
gratitude.closing: One more day. That's the whole thing.
